entry.title: Re: Users not able to review content from learning histor...
entry.content: Its already enabled

entry.title: Re: UUIDs and Unique Semantic Primary Keys
entry.content:
Hi Peter,

Thank you for your response. Maybe I was not clear in the description. I'm talking about a table with related keys, not ones generating new records. For example, an Order Partners table where I have the key (Order/Partner Function/Partner), and I have to guarantee that the Partner appears only once for the same Order/Partner Function.

entry.title: Re: How to perform Arithmetic operations in Subscription...
entry.content:
Hello,

The easiest way to do that is to use directly the "ratio" property which is configurable in the "calculate price element" component, there you can input the factor with which you want to multiply the returned price. See documentation here: https://help.sap.com/docs/PRICE_CALCULATION/53987d38467243e28cbeb8514d015666/0104c68a9a3146d8bb85de11d5e4b9d1.html

Have a great day,

Élodie Brenker
(PM @ Price Calculation)

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Meetup Oslo
entry.content:
I can't wait to attend! 🚀

Looking forward to an electrifying evening of networking, and tech talk with fellow SAP enthusiasts.✨ This is the perfect chance to connect in a relaxed setting.🎉

Eager to see who I'll meet!

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch #5 Innovant : SAP Supers heroes
entry.content:
Le jeudi 18 avril dernier, la communauté SAP parisienne a vécu une soirée digne des Avengers : la 5ème édition du Stammtisch, désormais une institution incontournable ! Avec 78 super-participants, l'événement, sponsorisé par Malt, s'est déroulé dans leurs locaux et a explosé les records d'affluence. Ce fut un véritable moment de partage et d'échanges enrichissants. Et comme si ce n'était pas suffisant, trois super-développeurs advocates venus tout droit des États-Unis ont enfilé leurs capes pour partager leur expertise et dévoiler les dernières technologies SAP.

La soirée a commencé avec une présentation introduisant les membres de l’équipe des SAP Developers Advocate. Trois héros du code ont fait le déplacement pour partager leur mission :

  1. Thomas Jung : Manager de l’équipe
  2. Rich Heilman : Développeur Advocate pour les sujets ABAP, Steampunk, RAP
  3. Kevin Riedelsheimer : Développeur Advocate pour les sujets Cloud Native, CAP

Au cœur de la soirée, les participants ont découvert Joule, une solution révolutionnaire développée par SAP. Cette IA est un véritable super-pouvoir que les développeurs peuvent acquérir pour les aider à créer des applications SAP.

L'une des caractéristiques les plus remarquables de Joule réside dans sa connaissance approfondie de l'environnement de travail du développeur. Elle est capable de fournir des solutions adaptées et personnalisées durant le processus de développement. D’une simple phrase, Joule peut générer une application Fiori.

La soirée s'est conclue par une discussion ouverte, offrant à tous les participants l'opportunité d'échanger sur une variété de sujets. Cela a permis à chacun de partager ses idées, ses connaissances et ses expériences, contribuant ainsi à enrichir l'événement et à en faire un succès.

Un grand merci à notre sponsor, Malt, sans qui cette 5ème édition du Stammtisch n'aurait pas été possible. Grâce à eux, combiné à la présence des 3 mousquetaires @thomas_jung , @RichHeilman, @KRiedelsheimer, et aux participants, cette soirée a été un véritable succès et restera gravée dans nos mémoires comme un moment d'exception. Ensemble, nous avons prouvé une fois de plus que la communauté SAP est plus forte que jamais !

entry.title: Can these two standard apps be extended-F0458 Access Approver and F0220 Request Access
entry.content: Can these two standard apps be extended-F0458 Access Approver and F0220 Request Access

entry.title: Re: is Odata supported in SAPME 15.5

hello, and one more question, is it possible to use Odata to collect the list of SFCs/Inventory_id for a specific item? because I can't found any functionality in Odata document to search the basic data or the sfc's connected by item?

thank you

Br,

Vian

entry.title: How to bind connectivity service in BTP in Hybrid Testing
entry.content:
Hello Colleague,

As I know to bind the connectivity service in BTP in Hybrid Testing, we need the SSH tunnel, but from https://pages.github.tools.sap/cap/docs/advanced/hybrid-testing#overwriting-service-credentials , only 

cds bind -2 my-service --credentials '{ "onpremise_proxy_host": "localhost" }'

is enough, may I know if we still need 

CF SSH

 to build the tunnel, because in our case, only add 

          "credentials": {
            "onpremise_proxy_host": "localhost"
          },

in  .cdsrc-private.json, we still meet the error 

Connection timed out: getsockopt: connectivityproxy.internal.cf.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com/52.86.131.53:20003

and in which release the command 

cds bind -2 my-service --credentials '{ "onpremise_proxy_host": "localhost" }'

is available, because we meet error 

Invalid option: --credentials

Thank you very much

entry.title: Re: Failed to commit objects to server : Undefined Info S...
entry.content:
Hello everybody, i think having find the solution with this SAP's note : 3437779 - Failed to commit objects to server : Undefined Info Store error while creating Users/Groups, logging in with LDAP authentication In BI 4.3 SP04

Best regards.

Lionel

entry.title: What's new for Workspaces in SAP Build Work Zone?
entry.content:
Recently new features were released to streamline the creation process and the member handling of workspaces in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition. Among these features you will find:

  • a new workspace creation dialog
  • the removal of external workspaces as a separate workspace type  
  • a consistent user experience between private and public workspaces
  • expanded access control concepts: external users can now access public and private workspaces as members

See also the information in the What's New Viewer from April 18, 2024. 

These are the major innovations for workspaces:

Removal of External Workspaces
You cannot create anymore “External Workspaces”. When you create a new workspace, you can choose between creating a public workspace or a private workspace:

What are public workspaces? Workspaces that all users can view, join and collaborate in. Members can be invited to join or they can search for the workspace using the search functionality or by browsing on the workspaces screen and then request to join. Also non-members can join a public workspace - depending on the settings defined by the administrator.

What are private workspaces? Workspaces that only those users can join, who are invited to the workspace. In general, private workspaces are hidden and can't be found with the search functionality. If you're not invited to a private workspace, you can't see it on the workspaces screen unless the workspace administrator has defined the setting to Allow users to request to join this workspace.

Migration of External Workspaces to Private Workspaces
Existing external workspaces will be migrated automatically to private workspaces. An external warning icon will be added to the workspace header to indicate that the content of this workspace is visible to external users:

Handling of External Users/Members
External users can now be invited to both private and public workspaces. External users are guests of an organization, and they should not have access to any confidential or internal information. Before external users can access the system, they must be created on Identity Authentication and be provisioned by the Identity Provisioning service to SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition. For more information, see About External Users.

This blog post guides you through the steps to create a private workspace. In general the creation flow is streamlined, and there is no big difference between creating private or public workspaces.

How to create a workspace?

  1. When creating a new workspace, choose "Private Workspace" or "Public Workspace". In this blog post we will focus on creating a private workspace:

2. Enter a unique name and a description. Select one of the templates or you can select No Template. 

Note: If the workspace is public, the description is visible for everybody in the search. When the workspace is private, the description is visible for members only after they’ve been invited to the workspace or join the workspace.

You can define if the workspace is activated on creation, or you activate it later.

Define the following settings:

  • External users can access the workspace and can be invited as members.
  • Allow all members to invite users. When not selected, only workspace admins can invite users.
  • Allow users to request to join this workspace (for private workspaces) or Allow others to join this workspace (for public workspaces).

 3. Click on Next to define the member participation settings. They can be read-only, limited or full. You also define the task, upload and approval policy:

4. Click Finish and the workspace is created.

5. You can change each of the settings in the edit mode of the workspace:

6. If needed you can change the member participation setting, for example you change the collaboration level from Full to Limited:

Additional Features for Public Workspaces

Collaboration Levels for Non-Members
A non-member visibility is available for workspaces to give non-members access to the workspace content. Workspace admins have the choice: (1) to give non-members the same collaboration settings as members or (2) to use a new read-only mode for non-members. This will hide the different membership & collaboration related features for the non-members across the workspace and its content. 

Access Control Settings 
Public workspaces now offer expanded access option to non-members, and offer these access options:

  • Specific users or user lists
  • Specific roles (for site admins)
  • All internal users
  • All external users (when enabled)
  • All users

If you need additional information on workspaces, you can access the SAP Help Portal:

How to create a workspace? 
How to manage workspaces? 
Workspace Settings

If you have more questions on SAP Build Work Zone, visit our FAQ page on SAP Community.

More Information on new Features and Functions

  • Check out the roadmap for standard & advanced edition.
  • You can find more information in the What is new Viewer for standard edition and advanced edition.
  • The availability date of the new features on the preview systems and the information about how to use them can be found in the What's New on Preview Systems.

Learn more

SAP Build Work Zone on: sap.com / SAP Community
Discovery Center missions: standard / advanced edition
SAP Build Work Zone on SAP PartnerEdge

Follow SAP Build on Social Media and Join the SAP Builders Group

  • Twitter (hashtag #SAPBuild)
  • LinkedIn

Join also the SAP Builders group and engage with a community of peers to unleash innovation by building apps, process automations, and creating business sites.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: MFBF - Restrict quantity field to 3 digits
entry.content: I don't find any BAdI for this, nevertheless you could try some implicit Enh Point in forms such as CHECK_QUANTITY of function group BARM (if form not executed, perform some debug or trace)

entry.content:
Hi @AmitKrSingh 

I have 1 requirement  in the Business Partner transaction.

Requirement 1 - In Business partner transaction , some address details( like street , city etc ) are masked on the basis of roles ( Attribute based Masking ). Now suppose I dont have  the role to view this value, Is it possible to edit this masked fields in BP transaction. We are in S4 hana system .

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello David,

My name is Pragathi and I am an SAP ABAP developer from India. I am writing to express my strong interest in participating in the SAP Digital Skills Initiative. As a middle-aged woman with four years of hands-on experience in SAP ABAP development, I am keen to expand my skills and explore new career growth opportunities through this esteemed program.

In addition to my technical skills, I bring a strong analytical mindset, a collaborative approach, and a dedication to achieving excellence in every project I undertake. I am excited about the opportunity to join the SAP Digital Skills Initiative and further my career with an SAP certification. Thank you for considering my application. I look forward to the next steps and am eager to start this transformative journey.

I have a requirement of creating a table in SAP HANA Cloud Database with end user's input from the UI. Is it possible to create a table without defining the entity in schema and also view that table in the UI using SAP Fiori elements. Please help me with your suggestion.

Thanks & Regards. 
Livingston Immanual.

entry.title: Re: UUIDs and Unique Semantic Primary Keys

Regards,

Wolfgang

entry.title: Orderby Query in Offline Mode for Object Tables
entry.content:
Hello!

I am encountering a strange issue when trying to sort the objects in my object table using the following query:
$expand=to_Result,to_ItemValues&$orderby=ItemNo&$filter=to_Result/WorkOrder eq '{MaintenanceOrder}' and to_Result/Operation eq '{MaintenanceOrderOperation}'
(I have also tried the 'orderyby' property at the end of the URL with the same result)

The order of the objects is controlled by a field in ODATA called 'ItemNo'. This is simply any number between 1 and 14. While online, the query behaves predictably and orders the objects in an arrangement starting at object #1 and ending at object #20. (1,2,3...12,13,14) (See Image #1)

However, when switching to offline mode the order changes to: 1,10,11,12,13,14,2,3,4,5,6,7,8,9 (See Image #2). I can see the logic that is being used here, and in a way it makes sense to a computer... but it is still incorrect.

Is there a way to get this to organize correctly without attempting to create an entire extension control? Is this a bug in the MDK editor itself, and how it reads number values offline?
(I have attached a copy of the section/object-table structure)

Any help would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: Re: How to delete lockbox in SAP S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, we don't have an option to delete the lockbox files that are already posted in the system. It is suggested to use the reversal option in the Reprocess Lockbox Items app to reverse the lockbox item.

  1. Go to Reprocess Lockbox Items app.
  2. Select the lockbox Items.
  3. Click on the Reverse button.
  4. A job will be scheduled to perform the reversal with the selected reversal reason and posting date.

entry.title: SAP Community Meetup Paris
entry.content:
📢Appel aux professionnels SAP à Paris !

Rejoignez-nous pour le prochain SAP Community Meetup (Pour les connaisseurs, anciennement "Stammtisch") - une soirée idéale pour se connecter, collaborer et partager des insights. Ne manquez pas cette occasion de réseauter, d'échanger des idées et de rester à la pointe des dernières stratégies et tendances SAP.

Nous sommes ravis et impatients d'accueillir Woody Zuill, un pionnier du Software Teaming (Mob Programming), pour notre 6ème édition. Il animera une discussion captivante sur "No Estimates."

Marquez vos calendriers pour le 4 juin. Hâte de vous voir ! 🗼

entry.title: Missing option how to reverse delivery distribution from EWM
entry.content:
We are missing an option on how to reverse the distribution of delivery from EWM. Problems occur when we want to change the amount on inbound delivery or batch on outbound delivery.

 

is there any option how to reverse delivery so we will be able to edit delivery in ERP (VL02/VL32)?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Missing option how to reverse delivery distribution f...
entry.content:
Hi,

There are two ways to reverse the process after distributing the delivery to EWM.

  1. You can delete/zero out the outbound delivery from EWM side with APP "Manage Outbound Delivery Orders" or Change Inbound Deliveries(APP ID F1706) then re-create the delivery.
  2. You can also enable the delivery change flag at customizing "Configure SAP EWM-Specific Parameters" (SSCUI 102212), then it is possible to change the delivery at LE side APP "Change Outbound Delivery" (APP ID VL02n) or "Change Inbound Delivery"(APP ID VL32n).

 

Best Regards,

entry.title: Re: MD04 shows Double Confirmation AB + LA for Scheduling agreement.
entry.content: Pls refer to notes: 2044484 - Troubleshoot to EKET, EKES, EKBE table inconsistencies

entry.title: Campaign auto restart after error - adjust timing
entry.content:
Hi,

I sometimes have campaigns that show an error in the logs such as:

Package "189" started at "28.05.2024, 07:43:24 (CET)" was finished with a fatal error.

When this happens, the campaign automatically restarts after roughly 30 minutes.

Is there some way to influence that 30 minute timeframe? We'd like to make this shorter. 

This is on an onprem SAP Marketing system.

entry.title: Re: Wily Introscope offline no matter what :-(
entry.content:
Which version do you have?

Version 10.8 have a slight difference regarding the installation procedures. Please check SAP note  3247270 - Introscope 10.8 Release Notes:

Please don't forget to update the Management Modules (without this procedure, SOLMAN will never be able to connect), and specially call the osgiindex.bat / osgiindex.sh scripts!

Other relevante SAP notes:

  • 2557578 - How to install/uninstall Introscope Enterprise Manager 10.x - Windows/Unix
  • 2512694 - How to correctly import/discover the Introscope Enterprise Manager (EM) into SAP Solution Manager
  • 3297242 - Support Introscope EM 10.8 on SOLMAN_SETUP
  • 2560977 - CA Introscope Enterprise Manager compatibility in SAP Solution Manager 7.1 / 7.2

entry.title: Re: Connecting SAP CAP with SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hello @TuncayKaraca,

Thank you for your help. You are absolutely right – I haven't considered the topic of OpenTelemetry so far. Have you worked with it before? I've looked at the documentation, but it's not entirely clear to me. Could you provide me with some more information?

I already opened a new question regarding this issue yesterday. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/how-to-use-opentelemetry-for-sap-cloud-foundry-cloud-alm/qaq-p/13713560/comment-id/4830923#M4830923  We are really struggling and hope you can assist us.

Thank you in advance!

Best regards

entry.title: Re: User master data extract program from S4 to Ariba giv...
entry.content:
Hi, 

 

This might be related to the missing mapping between SAP and Ariba fields. Check the configuration in SPRO->Intergration with other SAP components --> master data integration --> enhancements --> Maintain field map for master data or catalog upload request

entry.title: SAP Readiness Check from S/4HANA 1709 to S/4HANA 2023
entry.content:
We are looking to migrate our SAP to latest version so want for readiness check. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Readiness Check from S/4HANA 1709 to S/4HANA 2023
entry.content:
Dear customer,

please refer to SAP KBA 3404904 - Getting Started on SAP Readiness Check

The procedure on how to generate the Readiness Check for an S/4 HANA Upgrade is described in SAP Note 3059197 - SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA upgrades

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Where can I start learning SAP S/4HANA Utilities?
entry.content:
We  recommend to access our Executing Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Utilities learning journey

Overview
This learning journey, “Executing Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Utilities,” covers the SAP S/4HANA Utilities. ​This course will be released in several parts/updates in the next months. The first part covers the basic overview of SAP S/4HANA Utilities, introducing you to its primary operations, basic functions, and master data.

In the next releases, the Device and Work Management and Meter Reading will be covered with deeper insight into Energy Data Management, Machine Learning applications, and more. A few chapters cover the specifics of the German energy market, including SAP Market Communication, Master Data Updates, Intelligent Metering, Billing Enhancements, and EEG Billing. Targeted training on Contract Accounting, Convergent Invoicing, and Intelligent Metering will also be provided. This course is designed to offer you a broad overview and practical skills in SAP utilities business processes.​

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, a learner will

  • be able to navigate SAP S/4HANA Utilities
  • apply concepts of energy data management and financial accounting,
  • use machine learning applications for meter reading and billing,
  • handle area-specific aspects, especially for the German energy sector.

They will be equipped with skills in contract accounting, convergent invoicing, and intelligent metering. Additionally, they will gain experience in tailoring and using the software's key features for utility industries. 

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you.

We appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

entry.title: Feature Request: Allow descriptions for global Process Intelligence filters
entry.content:
Hi,

we would like to address the following feature request:

As an Analyst I would like to add a description to my global filters within my SAP Signavio Process Intelligence dashboard, so that I can provide the Consumers more easily with the information as of why certain default filters have been set in a certain way and how they can be adapted to achieve other analysis results.

BR, Anna

entry.title: Internal error in LMLCCS_UPDATEF02 LCL_DB method get_normvector position A with RC 1
entry.content:
Hi All,

While doing the MIRO, I'm getting the below error, Please suggest to resolve this error, We are using the S4 hana Public cloud.

Internal error in LMLCCS_UPDATEF02 LCL_DB method get_normvector position A with RC 1

Message no. MLCCS009

entry.title: Re: SAP Event Mesh - Message processing status "Retry" fo...
entry.content:
Hi Sriprasad,

Thanks for your reply. My question is specifically about the final message status in Cloud Integration Monitoring.

Once the retry is executed based on the value configured in the parameter "Max. Number of Retries", the message still shows as "Retry" status. Ideally the message status in Cloud Integration should change from "Retry" to "Failed" since the retry is not successful.

Also, even if the parameter "Max. Number of Retries" is configured as 0 when retry is not required, then also the message ends up in "Retry" status in Cloud Integration.

I am interested to know if there is any possible way to display the status as "Failed" in Cloud Integration for messages that are not successful corresponding to a scenario with Event Mesh as source system. 

Thank you for the support.

Regards, Sudarvizhi

entry.title: SAC - Blending NOT Supported in Optimized Design Mode
entry.content:
Does anyone have some idea for workaround to get same result as Blending in Optimized Design mode?

I guess there must be some alternative?

entry.title: Re: How to write back a calculated measure in data model in SAC Planning?
entry.content: Hi Eric, facing the same issue. Created a new model and a fixed measure on the second model but when trying to cross model copy using LINK Im getting an error saying: "Cannot use "XXX" measure since it is calculated or conversion measure". Do you have an example for a code I can use ?

entry.title: HCM process - employee number no values
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I hope you are fine!

I have following problem: When I want to start a HCM process in Fiori as a HR administrator, I can't seem to find out why I have no employees to select?

I am assigned as a manager in my org unit (Peter Best) and I am able to find these users when trying to start a different process under "my team processes"

What am I missing? I have no knowledge in HCM and I couldn't find anything in the SAP documentation about employees.. Thank you in advance!

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/c6c3ffd90792427a9fee1a19df5b0925/5c0a8a531c533d58e10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: SAP Build App After Deployed into BTP getting Error while Open App Error GET --500 Missing zone
entry.content: Hi All,

entry.title: Re: Getting dates between given date range
entry.content:
Dear all, thanks for this query ,thanks my efforts got reduced. this FM DAY_ATTRIBUTES_GET
it resolved my purpose and does exact same, as asked in query, just give start and end date, it will give List of ALL dates between given input 2 dates .. very gratefull.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build App After Deployed into BTP getting Error while Open App Error GET --500 Missing zone
entry.content: Hi All, what is the update on this ? i am also getting the same 500 internal server erro, please help me with resolution steps.

Hi Deborah,

Could you solve this? I am facing the same issue.

Thank you.

Best Regards,

entry.title: Re: Default Width of first table column in matrix question (c4c survey)
entry.content: Hi Deborah,

entry.title: Re: #COE#RFL01-Mass Retirement / Mass Transfer of fixed a...
entry.content:
There is no mass transfer option. 

For mass retirement refer to the API API_FIXEDASSETRETIREMENT_G4BA
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/5bcca49998834de2b5533c36602e52a2/2b3aafb1a2df4e6fa8610fe55a375449.html?locale=en-US

For acquisition API_FIXEDASSETACQUISITION_G4BA
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/5bcca49998834de2b5533c36602e52a2/0bb55e6d8c6a4353afc20d3542cbc508.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: what is the difference between adobe forms on public...
entry.content:
Hi,

You can go thru the blog https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/output-management-customize-content-form-for-outgoing-check/ba-p/1334012, which explains in detail, how output management works in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This will help you in identifying how Adobe forms are different from On-Premise

Thanks,
Prasanth

entry.title: Re: Default Width of first table column in matrix question (c4c survey)

Hi everyone,

i have the following problem when I click on apply in the eDocument cockpit I get the following message.
The following scenario: We are sending a domestic invoice. Our CIF NR is also displayed in the error message.
Has anyone here ever had the same problem and can help ?

entry.title: Re: Internal error in LMLCCS_UPDATEF02 LCL_DB method get_...
entry.content:
Hello @HemanthK 

Check the notes

  • 2492665 - Error MLCCS 009 in LMLCCS_UPDATEF02 LCL_DB, method get_normvector
  • 2482025 - MALENA: MLCCS 009 or missing cost component split for multilevel processes
  • 3042535 - Error MLCCS 003 or MLCCS 009 in goods movement posting
  • 2699241 - Error message MLCCS202 or MLCCS009 in S/4 HANA

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

br,

entry.title: Re: MD04 shows Double Confirmation AB + LA for Scheduling...
entry.content: Thanks for your reply. In order to apply permanent fix should we need to modify confirmation key configuration as it happening all the time?

entry.title: Re: Help with NoClassDefFoundError for com/sap/aii/securi...
entry.content:
Hi Syed,

while using NWDS add the jar files by this method shown below though build path.

 This should fix all dependencies automatically. 

In case you are adding external jars for example apache classes then dependencies need to be resolved by adding external jars to project path. 

Regards

Anupam

entry.title: EWM Setup in embedded system S4 2022 FPS 02 seems faulty
entry.content:
Hello experts,

we are struggling with some missing synchronous goods movements posting. Could be that the system cannot read the data propperly  from control cycle because is dows not find the right entry due to missing warehouse number.

in lpk1 forward navigation with staging method 5-ewm the entered psastage screen is not showing up with filled warehouse number. this is causing a lot of neg. side effects. i adjusted the supply chain unit  connection to the business partners. from a former consultancy this entry seemed incorrect. but the correction was no sufficient. what can i checke else? problem is quite urgent due to productive impact.

where does the warehouse number here come from?

entry.title: Re: Why my Service is not Available in Fiori
entry.content:
Hi @FKHAN123 try changing to V2, sometimes V4 are not displayed... 

Thanks-

Abhishek

entry.title: Re: Enable Now Web Assistant is covering the application...
entry.content:
Hi Anna

Have you enabled the "Hide In-App Help for Desktop on Running SAP Companion" option?

In Producer you can find it in Tools - Settings | SAP Companion: Playback Settings - Content and Behavior.

It means that when both Companion and Companion for Desktop are running the Companion Help Panel will take precedence over the display of the Companion for Desktop Sidebar.

entry.title: BADI For Dunning notice - SAP PUBLIC CLOUD
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

  We are trying to add custom fields logic for a dunning notice form but we could not able to get BADI name on public cloud, can you please suggest.

Form name :  FIN_FO_DUNN_NOTE

 

Regards,

Kathiravan S

SAP Forms service by Adobe SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: How to integrate SAP Digital Payments Add-On into a non-SAP e-commerce solution
entry.content:
@VVaquero_24 a lot changed between ECC 6.0 and S/4Hana, e.g. the data model was refactored.

Still I recommend asking your question on community.sap.com.

entry.title: Re: Conversion Routine in AMDP for MATERIAL (MATNR)
entry.content:
Solution that I found to be better and used:

I created a new CDS on MARA with this code:

SELECT FROM mara {
key matnr,
LTRIM(matnr, '0') AS Converted_matnr
}.

and used that CDS.

entry.title: Payment Due Date in 'GET' call of API - API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV
entry.content:
I have invoices interface using API - API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV where I need to send the Payment Due Date as well.
I know that in the standard SAP on-premise there is a function module “DETERMINE_DUE_DATE” to calculate this from the payment terms. Is there a CDS that has this information to be send from SAP system ?
Also, where can we write the code to implement this functionality in 'GET' call for the above API ?

entry.title: SBO Failed to get database information from SLDClient SLDClientOP::GetCommonDBCredentials Exception
entry.content:
Hello,

I have error when login SAP Business One. Here is error

2024-05-28 16:48:01.099675 Sys Err I Biz SLDClient SLDClientOP::GetCommonDBCredentials SLD Client Exception code:7 message:function return is empty : GetCommonDBCredential # # TID=9348 sldclient.cpp 1152 Customized=0
2024-05-28 16:48:01.113425 Sys Err I Biz Failed to get database information from System Landscape Directory # # TID=9348 MID=234109629 Area=Authorization Customized=0
2024-05-28 16:48:01.120284 Genl Err I Tec LOG_GetCommonDBCredentials : get common db credential failed. # # TID=9348 palogin.c 2026 Customized=0

Please help me

entry.title: The Crucial Role of Problem-Solving in Managing Reliability in Asset-Intensive Organizations
entry.content:
In the realm of asset-intensive industries, where equipment and machinery are pivotal to operations, ensuring reliability with minimum cost is paramount. At the heart of Managing Reliability lies a fundamental aspect: problem-solving. This critical element is something that everyone does every day in their work. Problem-solving is not merely about fixing issues as they arise. Rather, it encompasses a proactive approach to identify, analyze, and address potential problems before they escalate and are detected as functional failures. The organization's capability to solve problems accurately and within the business context is a key to reliable performance. Failure to understand the cause of a failure inevitably leads to more failures and losses.

One of the primary challenges in asset-intensive organizations is the sheer complexity, interconnectedness, and dependencies of systems. A breakdown in one component can have cascading effects, leading to downtime, increased maintenance costs, and compromised safety. Effective problem-solving acts as a preemptive measure against such scenarios, fostering a culture of continuous improvement and operational resilience.

A robust problem-solving framework involves several key stages and steps. It begins with identifying the problem and defining the problem statement. Once the problem statement is defined, one must prioritize and decide to work on the problem. The next step is thorough analysis, which involves collecting and analyzing data that aids in understanding the root cause of the problem. Based on the data analysis, solutions to mitigate the problem will be developed, and these solutions will be tested against the defined problem statement. Once testing is done, the implementation of the solutions comes next. To close the loop on an enterprise-wide level, it is important to monitor and validate performance improvement to close out the problem and share the learnings. The figure below shows the Problem-Solving methodology cycle.

  1. Identification – Operations, Maintenance, Threat Identification, and Safety Critical can help with problem identification.
  2. Definition – What is the actual expected impact? This question helps to define the problem.
  3. Prioritization & Decision – Quantify the risk and prioritize the problems to decide which problem needs to be addressed first.
  4. Understand the problem – Collect and analyze the data for the next phase. Also, understand the cause of the problem through causal reasoning and causal Learning. Perform FMEA and Value Stream mapping.
  5. Solution Development – Develop solutions to mitigate the problem. This can be in the form of projects, Management of Change (MOC) requests, Engineering Designs, Technical Expertise, and Standards development.
  6. Solution Implementation – Test the solutions against the problem statement and implement the solutions. Implementation involves planning, scheduling, and execution.
  7. Validate and Close – Monitor and validate the expected performance and improvement. Close out the problem and share what you learned within and outside the community for greater benefits and reliability excellence.

Techniques of Problem Solving

  1. Reliability Data Analysis – There is a list of core techniques, both qualitative and quantitative, available to support problem-solving. In terms of data analysis, the set of tools such as Pareto Analysis, Weibull Analysis, Crow-AMSAA, Fault Tree Analysis, Life Cycle Cost Analysis (LCCA), and RAMS modeling are quite useful to process collected data and provide actionable insights.
  2. FMEA – Apply this technique to identify all possible Failure Modes (FM), ways in which something might fail, in a design, a manufacturing or assembly process, or a product or service. This can be applied at the component, equipment, functional systems, and unit or asset level.
  3. Value Stream Mapping – This technique or management method helps visualize the end-to-end business process and identify interfaces, resources, the flow of information, and the time consumed. It also helps to identify opportunities for improvement process times to improve productivity and efficiency. This aids in the identification of opportunities for improvement, including bottlenecks, rework, load-leveling, queue times, reliability, yields, and processing times to improve productivity and efficiency.
  4. Causal Learning and Causal Reasoning – Causal Reasoning are the five WHY questions used in everyday problem-solving situations. Causal Learning provides a way to discover the causes of current performance, learn from the causes, and take action to improve future performance.

Expectations and contributions from multiple collaborative teams involved for Problem Solving

  1. Reliability Department – Staffed with experienced personnel skilled in advanced reliability data analysis and problem-solving techniques, the reliability team runs data analysis to aid the operations and maintenance team in investment decisions, risk mitigation, and bad actors' improvement. It is expected that reliability engineers spend 70% of their time focusing on proactive and long-term activities.
  2. Frontline Operations, Maintenance Staff, and Supervisors – This team encounters problems with the equipment before anyone else. It is expected they know the well-developed basic troubleshooting skills for their equipment and process scope and responsibility. Supervisors are expected to support and develop problem-solving skills continually and own the resolution of the problems frontline operations and maintenance staff encounter. Also, they should know how to access available staff resources to support the problems they encounter and be empowered to engage them when required during any phase of problem-solving. This team helps in day-to-day problem-solving.
  3. Exclusive Problem-Solving Team – There is a sponsor for each team, with extended leadership team members accountable for the team's performance. The team can be physically or virtually located in the same area. The team is resilient and is not affected by past failures. Also, this team remains impartial, delivering unbiased results while maintaining respect for others in the organization. This team keeps other team members involved and updated on the progress. Communication with other teams is the key to success. This team understands the benefit of true collaboration and teamwork without underestimating the importance of meeting as a group regularly.
  4. Operations and Maintenance Team – If the frontline operators need additional support to solve a problem, the multi-discipline is typically the next group to support the frontline team on the shop floor. Team members within this team can self-organize into the troubleshooting skills for their equipment and processes. Team members are expected to have skilled technical expertise for operations and failure modes that can affect the equipment and processes they operate and maintain. They know how to access and consult available subject matter expert resources to support team members on the problem they encounter and are empowered to engage SMEs as and when required.

Problem-solving is one of the key elements in managing reliability. It supports overall reliability management and operationalizing an Asset's reliability strategy. Continuous improvement is the hallmark of effective reliability management, and problem-solving serves as the engine driving this improvement cycle. Organizations that prioritize problem-solving as a core competency empower their teams to tackle challenges head-on, foster a culture of innovation, and ultimately enhance operational reliability in asset-intensive environments.

In conclusion, problem-solving is not just a skill but a strategic imperative for managing reliability in asset-intensive organizations. By embracing a proactive problem-solving mindset, leveraging robust frameworks, fostering collaboration, and embracing innovative technologies, organizations can navigate the complexities of asset management with agility, resilience, and sustained success.

entry.title: SAP HANA for BE and SAP UI5 for FE questions
entry.content:
Hi guys, I've tried to create a full-stack web using sap cap for backend and fiori ui5 for front end.

Things I've done:
Created a server using cds-routing-handlers to create my custom server base on cds' services.
Deploy the server and database to SAP HANA, this is my server url

https://vacation-srv-bogus-mouse-pq.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com. you can extend the endpoint (/public/PblUsers) to see my users table.

Now I have a problem to deploy the front end, I'm using yeoman to create the template by `yo easy-ui5 ts-app`
I can develop the front end easily but I don't know how to deploy it, there's a way to do it by `cf html5-push` but I only push it into a html5-repo app-runtime, and it also create a app-host.

is there away that I can deploy my front end to sap hana without abap environment?

entry.content:
Hi!

How can i retrieve data per the following headers:

Msg Type Debtor Cash Acct Dbtr Creditor Cash Acct Cdtr Date Main Ccy Main Amount Last Update By My Side Other Side Main Ref Remittance Info User Ref.

 

Thank you!

entry.title: No EWM Warehouse Number in PSASTAGE coming from LPK1
entry.content:
Hello,

we are missing the warehouse number when we jump from LPK1 with staging indicator 5-ewm to PSASTAGE. There, the warehouse number is greyed out and therefore synchr. goods moevement cannot read any bin info from control cycles and fail.

 

any clue where this warehouse number is coming from  in regards to basis setup? checked already SCU etc. cogi then says: please enter warehouse number.

 

if i enter tcode /SCWM/PSASTAGE or /SCWM/PSASTAGE2 directly, warehouse number and entries are there but there seems to be a broken linkage coming from erp side (lkp1) which should also work and might be the reason for wrong/missing synchr. movements.

entry.title: UI5 App deployment error in S/4 Hana Public Cloud System
entry.content:
We have created the Communication Arrangement for Scenario SAP_COM_0104. We are using API_PRODUCTION_ORDER_2_SRV and API_PROD_ORDER_CONFIRMATION_2_SRV in our custom UI5 app for fetching and posting the data in the S/4 HANA Public Cloud. We have maintained the destination for this API. The application is working perfectly fine. We are trying to deploy the custom app to the same S/4 HANA Public Cloud system from which we are fetching the data, but it is failing to deploy.  We tried to deploy the application in both development and customizing tenant.

 

--- Steps to Reproduce ---

  1. Create Communication Arrangement for Scenario SAP_COM_0104.
  2. Maintain the destination in BTP, and check the connection
  3. Goto BAS and create the Fiori App using the template, select the system as the destination created in the previous step. Select the entity and create the app. 
  4. Open integrated terminal in the app and execute command "npm run deploy".
  5. Deploy will fail.

 

@SAPSupport @Ivan-Mirisola @Edrilan_Berisha

entry.title: Re: Vermont Child Care Contribution Tax Effective 7/1/2024
entry.content:
SAP Planned Delivery Date
May 31, 2024

entry.title: Re: Change Column name on my Report
entry.content:
Look all my ABAP Code for my Report : 

REPORT mm_stock_report.      

TABLES : kna1, knb1, mvke, mara, mard, TVM1T , TVM4T.     


SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK sel_block WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-s00.  
  PARAMETERS     : plant  TYPE bukrs OBLIGATORY DEFAULT '',            
                 SALS_ORG TYPE vkorg OBLIGATORY DEFAULT '',
                 MAT_GRP TYPE matkl.

SELECT-OPTIONS : cr_date2 FOR mara-ersda,      
                 matnr    FOR mard-matnr.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK sel_block.               

DATA:gr_alv       TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table,
     gr_functions TYPE REF TO cl_salv_functions_list.


  SELECT mara~matnr,maktx,marc~werks,mard~lgort, mard~labst ,meins , /CWM/LABST,EINME, /cwm/valum,BWESB,TRAME, 
TVM1T~BEZEI, TVM4T~BEZEI AS PRGRP
    FROM mara
    INNER JOIN makt on makt~matnr = mara~matnr and makt~spras = 'G'   
    LEFT JOIN mvke ON mvke~matnr = mara~matnr AND mvke~vkorg = @SALS_ORG 
    LEFT JOIN marc ON marc~matnr = mara~matnr AND marc~werks = @plant   
    LEFT JOIN mard ON mard~matnr = mara~matnr AND marc~werks = @plant   
    LEFT JOIN TVM1T ON TVM1T~MVGR1 = mvke~MVGR1 AND marc~werks = @plant   
    LEFT JOIN TVM4T ON TVM4T~MVGR4 = mvke~MVGR4 AND marc~werks = @plant   

      WHERE marc~werks = @plant
      AND mvke~vkorg = @SALS_ORG
      AND mara~matkl = @Mat_grp
      AND mara~ersda IN @CR_date2
      AND mard~matnr IN @matnr
      INTO TABLE @DATA(mmm).                                            


  CALL METHOD cl_salv_table=>factory
    IMPORTING
      r_salv_table = gr_alv
    CHANGING
      t_table      = mmm.


gr_functions = gr_alv->get_functions( ).
gr_functions->set_all( abap_true ).
gr_alv->display( ).

entry.title: Re: country grouping Romania (61 or RO)
entry.content:
The note 1022651 - T500L: Incorrect molga for Romania; changing molga 47 says:

...

1) The country-specific development for Romania (the XX-CSC-RO-HR, HR-CEE components) uses the value 61 as a personnel country grouping,

...

Regards

Johnatan
